title: Melampyrum oligocladum
taxonomy: Melampyrum oligocladum is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobancheaceae. It is native to Europe and has been introduced to other parts of the world. The plant is a hemiparasitic, meaning it obtains some of its nutrients by drawing them from the roots of other plants. Melampyrum oligocladum is often found growing in woodland areas and is known to be a member of the Melampyrum genus. The species is a rare and endangered plant due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.
